text: is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语农业报道。

text: Sorghum is an important grain for Africa.
zh: 高粱是非洲重要的粮食作物。

text: Africa.
zh: 非洲。

text: Millions of Africans have more to eat because of Gabisa ajeta.
zh: 由于加比萨·埃杰塔（Gabisa Ejeta）的贡献，数百万非洲人有了更多食物。

text: The Ethiopian scientist developed sorghum seeds that can resist long dry periods.
zh: 这位埃塞俄比亚科学家培育出能够抵御长期干旱的高粱种子。

text: The seeds can also resist the striga weed, a big cause of crop failures in Africa.
zh: 这种种子还能抵抗独脚金杂草，这种杂草是非洲农作物歉收的一大主因。

text: Now, his work has earned him this year's World Food Prize from the World Food Prize Foundation in Des Moines, Iowa.
zh: 如今，他的研究成果使他荣获了今年由美国爱荷华州得梅因世界粮食奖基金会颁发的世界粮食奖。

text: Secretary of State Hillary Clinton made the announcement in Washington in June.
zh: 国务卿希拉里·克林顿六月在华盛顿宣布了这一消息。

text: She pointed out that Professor Ejeta did not just develop the seeds.
zh: 她指出，埃杰塔教授不仅培育出了这种种子。

text: He also worked to get them to farmers.
zh: 他还致力于将种子推广给农民。

text: He will receive the $250 ,000 award at a ceremony in October.
zh: 他将于十月在颁奖典礼上获得25万美元的奖金。

text: He is only the second African to win the prize since it was established in 1986.
zh: 自1986年该奖项设立以来，他是第二位获奖的非洲人。

text: Monty Jones, a rice expert from Sierra Leone, was the winner in 2004.
zh: 来自塞拉利昂的水稻专家蒙蒂·琼斯（Monty Jones）是2004年的获奖者。

text: Gabisa Ejeta is a professor at Purdue University in Indiana.
zh: 加比萨·埃杰塔是美国印第安纳州普渡大学的教授。

text: Over the years he has worked with farmers and seed companies and developed more than 80 seed types for Africa.
zh: 多年来，他与农民和种子公司合作，为非洲培育出80多种种子。

text: In the early 1980s, Professor Ejeta developed the first sorghum hybrid seeds.
zh: 20世纪80年代初，埃杰塔教授培育出第一批高粱杂交种子。

text: These resisted drought and led to a major increase in production.
zh: 这些种子能够抗旱，并大幅提高了产量。

text: Drought is not the only enemy.
zh: 干旱并不是唯一的敌人。

text: Stryga is a parasitic weed that Africans commonly call witchweed.
zh: 独脚金是一种寄生杂草，非洲人常称之为“巫婆草”。

text: The plant attacks sorghum and other crops and steals water and nutrients from the roots.
zh: 这种植物侵袭高粱等作物，从根部窃取水分和养分。

text: In the 1990s,
zh: 在20世纪90年代，

text: Gabisa Ejeta and another Purdue researcher, Larry Butler, identified the complex relationships between striga and sorghum plants.
zh: 加比萨·埃杰塔和普渡大学的另一位研究员拉里·巴特勒（Larry Butler）发现了独脚金与高粱之间复杂的相互关系。

text: plants.
zh: 植物。

text: That finding led to the development of seeds resistant to both striga and drought.
zh: 这一发现促成了既能抗独脚金又能抗旱的种子的培育。

text: Gbisa Ejeta was raised in a one -room hut in a rural village in west -central Ethiopia.
zh: 加比萨·埃杰塔在埃塞俄比亚中西部一个乡村的单间土屋里长大。

text: His mother wanted him to get an education.
zh: 他的母亲希望他能接受教育。

text: He walked 20 kilometers to school in a neighboring town.
zh: 他步行20公里到邻镇的学校上学。

text: He left home on Sunday nights and returned on Friday.
zh: 他每逢周日晚上离家，周五才回来。

text: For secondary school, he attended an agricultural and technical school.
zh: 中学时期，他就读于一所农业技术学校。

text: It was established by Oklahoma State University under an American government program.
zh: 这所学校是俄克拉荷马州立大学根据美国政府一项计划创办的。

text: From there, he received an invitation to study at Purdue, where he earned a doctoral degree.
zh: 从那里，他受邀前往普渡大学深造，并获得了博士学位。

text: But Professor Ejeta has never forgotten his African roots.
zh: 但埃杰塔教授从未忘记自己的非洲根源。

text: Today, he encourages other scientists to turn their attention to Africa's needs.
zh: 如今，他鼓励其他科学家把目光投向非洲的需求。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: 以上就是美国之音特别英语农业报道。

text: Transcripts and MP3s of our reports are at voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: 我们报道的文字稿和MP3音频可在voaspecialenglish.com网站上获取。

text: Thank
zh: 谢谢。
